name: KSHV_terminal_repeat
description: KSHV terminal repeats: 35-45 tandem copies of a ~801 bp GC-rich (~85% GC) unit flanking the unique long region. Contains the LANA-binding sites LBS1/LBS2 and the latent origin of replication (ori-P-like). TR fusion preserves repeat count on circularization, providing a clonality marker analogous to EBV TR. GC content and repetitiveness make short-read mapping into TR essentially uninformative — the TR is the canonical short-read graveyard of the KSHV genome.
expected_copy_count: 35-45 TR units per genome end; clonality preserved in episome
